skill.name: slack-gif-creator
skill.description: Create animated GIFs and emoji animations optimized for Slack, using Python animation primitives, visual effects, and validators for Slack size constraints.
skill.summary: Create and validate Slack-ready animated GIFs and emoji reactions.
skill.author: Cam10001110101

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many high-risk patterns, but review found they are false positives from animation terms, Apache license text, and Python examples. No evidence of malicious networking, credential access, command execution, data exfiltration, or prompt injection was found. Residual risk is low because the skill writes and reads local GIF files and documents a package installation command.

low_findings.title: Local GIF File Read and Write Operations
low_findings.description: The toolkit saves generated GIFs to caller-provided paths and validates existing GIF paths. This is expected for a GIF creation library, but users should choose output paths carefully to avoid overwriting local files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code directly uses caller-provided paths for saving and validating GIF files. The behavior is intentional and local, with no evidence of broad filesystem traversal or destructive operations.

low_findings.title: Documented Package Installation Command
low_findings.description: The skill documentation includes a pip install command for runtime dependencies. This is legitimate setup guidance, but it can download packages and modify the active Python environment.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command is clearly documented as dependency setup and does not include shell interpolation or hidden arguments. The remaining risk is normal package installation risk.

low_findings.title: Template Import Path Modification
low_findings.description: Animation templates append the parent skill directory to Python import paths before importing local modules. This supports local examples, but import path changes can affect module resolution when run in unusual directories.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path append targets the skill parent directory and is used for local imports. It is a real behavior with limited risk and no evidence of malicious import hijacking.

content.user_title: Create Slack-Ready GIFs and Emoji Animations
content.value_statement: Slack GIFs often fail because size, dimensions, and frame counts are hard to balance. This skill provides animation primitives and validators for Slack-ready GIFs and emoji.
actual_capabilities: Creates animated GIF frames with reusable Python animation primitives.
Provides templates for shake, bounce, fade, spin, zoom, morph, and movement effects.
Validates Slack emoji and message GIF size and dimension constraints.
Optimizes GIF output through color limits, duplicate frame removal, and frame-count guidance.
Renders text, emoji, shapes, gradients, shadows, particles, and impact effects.
limitations: It does not upload GIFs to Slack or manage Slack workspace settings.
It requires Python image libraries such as Pillow, imageio, and numpy.
Strict emoji size limits may still require manual iteration and simpler designs.
Emoji and font rendering can vary across operating systems and installed fonts.

use_cases.title: Create Team Reaction Emoji
use_cases.description: Produce compact animated emoji reactions that fit Slack size limits.

use_cases.title: Prototype Motion Concepts
use_cases.description: Test motion ideas with reusable animation templates before creating final brand assets.

use_cases.title: Generate Slack GIF Utilities
use_cases.description: Build repeatable Python workflows for creating and validating Slack-ready GIF assets.

prompt_templates.title: Simple Reaction GIF
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for one Slack reaction.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Slack emoji GIF of a happy face bouncing twice. Keep it under 64KB and validate the final file.

prompt_templates.title: Message GIF With Text
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request for a larger Slack message GIF.
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a 480x480 Slack GIF that says "Launch day" with a subtle zoom, confetti effect, and readable outlined text.

prompt_templates.title: Reusable Animation Variant
prompt_templates.prompt: Create three Slack-ready emoji GIF variants using shake, pulse, and spin effects. Compare file size and visual clarity.

prompt_templates.title: Optimized Multi-Phase Animation
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with sequencing and optimization constraints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a multi-phase Slack GIF with anticipation, impact, and reaction. Use easing, reduce duplicate frames, and report optimization tradeoffs.

output_examples.input: Create a celebratory Slack GIF for a product launch.
output_examples.output: A square animated GIF with outlined launch text, confetti particles, a short bounce, and Slack size validation notes.

output_examples.input: Make an emoji GIF of a nervous face shaking.
output_examples.output: A compact emoji animation with horizontal shake motion, reduced colors, limited frames, and a final size check for Slack emoji upload.

output_examples.input: Turn a simple idea into three motion options.
output: A bounce version for playful tone.
A spin version for high-energy reactions.

best_practices: Start with fewer frames and colors when targeting Slack emoji uploads.
Validate file size after each meaningful change to avoid late redesign work.
Use outlines or shadows for text so it remains readable at small sizes.
anti_patterns: Do not use long animations for emoji GIFs because they often exceed 64KB.
Do not rely on gradients when file size is the main constraint.
Do not skip final Slack validation before sharing or uploading the GIF.

faq.question: Can this skill upload GIFs directly to Slack?
faq.answer: No. It creates and validates GIF files, but Slack upload remains a separate manual or API step.

faq.question: What size does it target for Slack emoji?
faq.answer: It targets 128x128 emoji GIFs and helps keep them near Slack's strict 64KB limit.

faq.question: Can it create larger message GIFs?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports larger message GIFs, commonly around 480x480 with higher frame counts.

faq.question: Does it require Python dependencies?
faq.answer: Yes. The toolkit uses image libraries such as Pillow, imageio, and numpy.

faq.question: Can I combine animation effects?
faq.answer: Yes. The examples show composing movement, shake, bounce, text, particles, and easing effects.

faq.question: Will every emoji GIF fit under 64KB automatically?
faq.answer: No. The validators report size issues, but complex animations may need fewer frames, colors, or elements.
